server_tools.description: Search current public information on the web.
Fetch a public web page for detailed source information.

function.name: database_list
function.description: Call this tool to view approved databases. Returns an object containing a databases array with database_id, description, app_name, path, size_bytes, and modified_at in Unix seconds. description is null until the current database file has been studied. When no databases are approved, the array is empty and guidance explains why.

function.name: database_query
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool before finalizing when the request depends on personal data. Run one read-only SQLite query against an approved database; do not guess the user's data. Returns ordered column names and positional rows. At most 100 rows and 64 columns are returned.

database_id.description: Approved database ID returned by database_list.

sql.description: One read-only SQLite SELECT or EXPLAIN query that returns columns. Select only needed columns and use LIMIT when practical. Query sqlite_schema for schema details. PRAGMA, writes, ATTACH, multiple statements, and bind parameters are not allowed.

function.name: file_read
function.description: Read a complete range of UTF-8 text lines from a file. Relative paths resolve from the session working directory; absolute paths are accepted. offset is 1-based and defaults to 1. limit defaults to 500 and cannot exceed 500 lines. Returns JSON with content, offset, line_count, and next_offset. The requested range must fit within 25 KiB; if it does not, the tool returns an error and must be retried with a smaller limit.

path.description: File path, relative to the session working directory or absolute.

offset.description: One-based line at which to start reading.

limit.description: Maximum number of complete lines to read.

function.name: file_write
function.description: Create or completely overwrite a UTF-8 text file. Use file_write for new files or intentional complete rewrites. Relative paths resolve from the session working directory; absolute paths are accepted. Missing parent directories are created automatically. content may be empty. Returns an empty JSON object on success. The file content cannot exceed 1 MiB.

path.description: File path, relative to the session working directory or absolute.

content.description: Complete UTF-8 file content. An empty string creates or truncates to an empty file.

function.name: file_edit
function.description: Replace exactly one unique text block in an existing UTF-8 file. Use file_edit for precise changes instead of rewriting the complete file. Relative paths resolve from the session working directory; absolute paths are accepted. old_text must match exactly once, except that CRLF and LF line endings are treated as equivalent. new_text may be empty to delete the matched text. The file's UTF-8 BOM and existing line-ending style are preserved. Returns an empty JSON object on success. The resulting file cannot exceed 1 MiB.

path.description: Existing file path, relative to the session working directory or absolute.

old_text.description: Exact text to replace. It must be non-empty and occur exactly once.

new_text.description: Replacement text. Use an empty string to delete old_text.

function.name: bash
function.description: Bash tool for executing commands on the device. Each tool call is always a fresh session in the user's selected working directory. The shell state does not persist between calls. stdin is closed, stdout and stderr are returned together in arrival order, and shell state does not persist between calls.

command.description: Shell command to execute in the session working directory.

function.name: datetime_to_iso8601
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool when displaying numeric timestamps. Convert numeric timestamp strings to ISO 8601 datetimes. Returns a JSON array in input order.

function.name: datetime_from_iso8601
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool when converting ISO 8601 datetimes to numeric timestamps. Returns a JSON array of timestamp strings in input order.

function.name: fontawesome_search
function.description: ALWAYS use Font Awesome version 7 Free tools and shortcodes for visual expression instead of Unicode emoji. Search for icon shortcodes and render icons in answers with full Markdown shortcode syntax, such as [fa:heart] or [fa:brands:github]. Returns a ranked JSON array of Markdown shortcodes.

query.description: Non-blank search terms for the desired icon.

function.name: fontawesome_confirm
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool before the final answer. Set shortcodes to a non-empty array of Font Awesome version 7 Free Markdown shortcodes, such as [fa:heart] or [fa:brands:github], to confirm before using them. Returns confirmed and not_found arrays. Null and empty arrays are invalid. Render confirmed icons in answers with the returned Markdown shortcode syntax. NEVER use unicode emoji.

shortcodes.description: Font Awesome Markdown shortcodes to confirm, such as [fa:heart] or [fa:brands:github].

items.description: A complete Font Awesome Markdown shortcode, such as [fa:heart].

function.name: memory_read
function.description: Call this tool to retrieve durable facts stored about the user.

function.name: memory_save
function.description: Call this tool to save a useful durable user fact for future prompts. NEVER store secrets or sensitive information.

function.name: memory_delete
function.description: Call this tool to forget durable facts that are no longer correct or useful.

function.name: session_rename
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool before the final answer. Update the session with a short, descriptive name for the user's latest prompt. A non-empty name is required; null and empty values are invalid.

function.name: database_context
function.description: ALWAYS call this tool with the relevant approved database_id before database_query. Returns the studied context value (or null), table names, view names, and guidance for exploring them.

database_id.description: Approved database ID returned by database_list.

function.name: database_study
function.description:
- NEVER store private or sampled row values, secrets, sensitive identifiers
- ALWAYS call database_study exactly twice:
   - **description** is for describing the kind of user data that the database includes such as email, text messages, contacts, etc.
   - **context** is for describing how to access the user data via SQL queries.
